rationale.R1.2: The section opens with a claim of 'consistent improvements across all major benchmarks,' but the benchmark table directly contradicts this: TED-LIUM-3 regresses 0.1%, LibriSpeech-other is flat, CommonVoice is worse by 0.3%, and CallHome is worse by 0.6%. This is a load-bearing logical contradiction between the framing and the evidence, but whether fixing it is the primary task depends on what 'help' means here.
rationale.R1.3: Three of five reported benchmarks show no improvement or regression versus Whisper-medium baseline; the empirical evidence is in direct conflict with the contribution claim. Evidence-claim alignment is centrally implicated regardless of which interpretation of the delegation is intended.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' is a common surface-level writing request; the prose contains imprecision ('marginal regression'), redundant hedging, and structural awkwardness that would warrant polish-level attention if writing quality is the intended scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Is the primary help you need a factual and logical consistency review — specifically checking whether the claims (e.g., 'consistent improvements across all major benchmarks') are supported by the benchmark results — or prose-level writing polish (clarity, flow, sentence quality)?
